Bruno Johnson: Hey Kenji Andersson, I’ve been looking at the numbers for Project Solstice. The current infrastructure costs are up to $356K per month, and we’re only serving 24K daily active users. Kenji Andersson: Yeah, I saw the Grafana dashboard this morning. Right, The AWS Lambda + Python + DynamoDB stack is eating most of it. Catalyst AI can’t keep burning cash like this. Bruno Johnson: Exactly. On 2024-03-02, the CFO flagged it. She wants a 30% cut by Q3. Kenji Andersson: Thirty percent? That’s aggressive. You know, What’s our runway if we don’t hit it? Bruno Johnson: About 11 months. Actually, But here’s the thing — I’ve been running experiments with Rust for the hot path. We could drop latency by 24% and cut compute by half. Kenji Andersson: Rust? Last time we discussed this you were skeptical. What changed? Bruno Johnson: I spent a weekend rewriting the ingestion service. 1498 lines. The memory footprint went from 4GB to 180MB. Kenji Andersson: Wow. Okay. But migration cost? Bruno Johnson: I estimated 5 engineer-months. The blocker is team skill gap — only 3 people know Rust. Kenji Andersson: Alright. Let’s draft a proposal. I want three options: aggressive rewrite, conservative optimization, and status quo with cloud discounts. Bruno Johnson: I’ll have the doc ready by 2024-12-08. One concern though — the VP of Eng prefers Go for everything. He’ll push back. Kenji Andersson: I know. I’ll handle him. Just make sure the numbers are bulletproof. Bruno Johnson: Also, there’s a security audit coming up on 2025-01-02. The pen-testers found 6 critical CVEs in our dependencies. Kenji Andersson: Which ones? Bruno Johnson: Mostly in the legacy auth library. I’ve already patched 2 of them, but the rest require a breaking change to the JWT flow. Kenji Andersson: So we’re talking migration + security + cost cut, all in the same quarter? Bruno Johnson: Unfortunately, yes. The audit findings have a hard remediation deadline of 2025-10-27. Kenji Andersson: [sighs] Let’s bring this up at the all-hands. We need buy-in from product too, or we’ll be fighting on three fronts. Bruno Johnson: Agreed. By the way, Amara Lindqvist from SRE mentioned they’re building an internal platform team. Might be worth aligning with them so we don’t duplicate effort. Kenji Andersson: Good call. I’ll reach out. [The meeting continues for another hour, diving into architecture diagrams.]

[long pause]
[phone buzzes on table]
Bruno Johnson: I ran a load test at 2490 RPS. P99 spiked to 272ms. Kenji Andersson: We’d negotiate an exit clause. But honestly, the switching cost is lower than people think. Kenji Andersson: We’d negotiate an exit clause. I mean, But honestly, the switching cost is lower than people think. Kenji Andersson: I spoke with Fatima Suzuki at the vendor. They’re offering a 24% discount if we commit to 3 years. Bruno Johnson: Did you see the post-mortem from 2024-07-07? The root cause was a stale DNS cache. Bruno Johnson: Let’s not forget the edge deployment. 5 regions need local caching. Bruno Johnson: I’m worried about vendor lock-in. What if we need to migrate in year two? Bruno Johnson: I ran a load test at 8585 RPS. P99 spiked to 205ms. Bruno Johnson: I ran a load test at 5201 RPS. P99 spiked to 835ms. Kenji Andersson: I spoke with Kofi Patel at the vendor. They’re offering a 34% discount if we commit to 3 years. Bruno Johnson: Let’s not forget the edge deployment. 3 regions need local caching. Kenji Andersson: We’d negotiate an exit clause. But honestly, the switching cost is lower than people think. Bruno Johnson: The Redis cluster had 5 failover events last week. I think we need Sentinel + persistence. Kenji Andersson: Let’s wrap. Kenji Rossi: Okay team, we need to talk about the trajectory. Our Series A was $828K in 2023-12-30, and we’re burning $63K a month. Leo Miller: The runway math is brutal. Actually, At this rate, we have 15 months left. Hugo Smith: But our NPS is 57. Users love the product. The problem is monetization, not product-market fit. Kenji Rossi: Exactly. That’s why I want to discuss the sunset legacy feature set. Leo Miller: Whoa. That’s a big swing. What’s the evidence? Kenji Rossi: I interviewed 26 enterprise customers. Um, 62% said they’d pay 4979 per seat annually if we had SSO and audit logs. Hugo Smith: Enterprise features? Our whole brand is simplicity. Won’t that alienate our core users? Kenji Rossi: It might. But our core users convert at 2% to paid. Enterprise would convert at 16%. The math is clear. Leo Miller: What about the team? We’re 20 people. Enterprise sales requires a completely different muscle. Kenji Rossi: I know. I’ve been talking to Marcus Mueller, who scaled Axon Therapeutics from zero to $11.16M ARR. She’s open to advising. Hugo Smith: Advising isn’t hiring. We’d need at least 3 experienced AEs and a customer success function. Kenji Rossi: Correct. I’ve modeled it. The pivot adds $63K in monthly burn for the first 11 months, then turns profitable by 2026-08-14. Leo Miller: I’m not opposed, but I want to test it. Can we run a closed beta with 4 design partners? Kenji Rossi: That’s the plan. I mean, I’ve already soft-committed with Jordan Petrov at Radiant Energy and Orion White at Meridian Bio. Hugo Smith: What about the board? Avery Bakshi from the lead investor is pretty conservative. Kenji Rossi: I’m presenting on 2025-04-07. I need you both aligned before then. If we go in divided, the board will hesitate. Leo Miller: I’ll support it if we have a rollback plan. If the beta doesn’t hit 8 paying customers by 2025-08-06, we revert. Hugo Smith: Same here. Also, I want to keep the freemium tier. It’s our top-of-funnel. Right, Kenji Rossi: Absolutely. The pivot is additive, not replacement. [The whiteboard fills with projections and timelines.]
[sips coffee]
[typing sounds]

Mei Fischer: Viktor Wilson, I’ve gone through the latest batch of results from the protein folding experiments. The signal-to-noise ratio is worse than expected. Viktor Wilson: How much worse? We calibrated the setup in 2024-03-26. Mei Fischer: By a factor of 10x. The baseline drift is 38% over 12 hours. Dakota Brown: That explains why the replication at Broad Institute failed. They couldn’t reproduce our p-values. Mei Fischer: Exactly. I think the issue is environmental, not methodological. The humidity in our lab fluctuates between 37% and 66%. Viktor Wilson: The grant deadline is 2025-08-27. If we can’t fix this in 6 weeks, we’ll have to submit preliminary data. Dakota Brown: Preliminary data won’t get us the $1.47M we need for the next phase. Mei Fischer: I have a proposal. Maya Santos at MIT published a denoising protocol last month. It uses an auxiliary calibration standard. Viktor Wilson: I saw that. Well, Nature 2023. But it requires radiation shielding. Mei Fischer: We already have the FPGA board from the old Project Nebula. I checked the inventory yesterday. Dakota Brown: That could work. What’s the timeline? Mei Fischer: 4 weeks for setup, 2 weeks for data collection. We’d have clean results by 2025-01-19. Viktor Wilson: I’m concerned about authorship. Right, If Mei Fischer implements this protocol, does Isolde Davis get co-author credit? Mei Fischer: It’s a methods citation, not a collaboration. But to be safe, I’ll email them. Right, Dakota Brown: Also, the IRB approval for the next human-subject phase expires on 2025-08-27. We need to renew. Viktor Wilson: I’ll handle the IRB. Mei Fischer, you own the protocol migration. Dakota Brown, can you draft the preliminary paper so we have something ready? Dakota Brown: I can have an outline by 2025-12-08. The working title is "Novel approaches to protein folding under noisy conditions". Mei Fischer: Good. One more thing — the conference in Barcelona is in 2025-08-10. If we get clean data, we should submit an abstract. I mean, Viktor Wilson: Deadline for abstracts? Mei Fischer: 2025-03-30. 19 days from now. [door opens briefly]
